skill.name: lesson-builder
skill.description: Helps users build lesson outlines and teaching materials through guided discussion.
skill.summary: Turn course ideas into structured outlines and lesson materials through collaborative planning.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences that show lesson-planning workflow diagrams, not shell or Ruby execution. The weak cryptography and entropy findings are also false positives; no cryptographic code, obfuscated payload, prompt injection, network access, or file access was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged external command execution at markdown code fences, weak cryptography at metadata and recommendation text, and high entropy at the start of SKILL.md. Review found only lesson-planning instructions and workflow diagrams, with no executable commands, cryptographic algorithms, obfuscated content, or prompt injection text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations contain YAML metadata, Chinese lesson guidance, and fenced workflow diagrams. There is no evidence of executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, or hidden payload in the reviewed file.

content.value_statement: Many instructors have course ideas but lack a clear lesson structure. This skill guides a discussion that turns those ideas into an outline and teaching materials.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a two-step outline and courseware workflow.
Asks clarifying questions about audience, goals, materials, and format.
Creates a course outline with modules, timing, preparation tasks, and materials.
Drafts lesson content after the outline is confirmed.
Supports fast co-creation or stricter step-by-step confirmation.
limitations: Does not verify subject accuracy against external sources.
Does not create slide files or visual design assets directly.
Works best when the user provides topic, audience, and course goals.
Does not replace pedagogical review by an experienced instructor.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Workshop Quickly
use_cases.description: Turn a rough topic into a timed workshop outline and lesson draft.

use_cases.title: Organize Existing Teaching Notes
use_cases.description: Convert scattered notes into modules, learning goals, and learner-facing material.

use_cases.title: Refine a Course Before Delivery
use_cases.description: Review an existing outline and improve flow, scope, and required preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Lesson Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare a lesson about [topic] for [audience]. I need an outline first.

prompt_templates.title: Create Outline and Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a [duration] course on [topic]. Use fast co-creation and produce an outline, then lesson materials after I confirm it.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this course outline and help me improve the structure, timing, learner outcomes, and preparation list: [outline].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Detailed Teaching Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Use strict confirmation mode to build a course for [audience]. Include modules, timing, examples, demo steps, summary points, and homework.

output_examples.input: Prepare a 60 minute onboarding lesson for new support agents.
output_examples.output: A course outline with a clear learning goal, four timed modules, preparation tasks, required tools, and a confirmation step before drafting courseware.

output_examples.input: Turn my rough AI safety notes into a workshop.
output_examples.output: A structured workshop plan that groups the notes into modules, identifies learner outcomes, suggests examples, and lists missing details to confirm.

output_examples.input: Write detailed materials after I approve the outline.
output_examples.output: Lesson content organized by module, with opening remarks, concepts, examples, demonstration steps, summaries, and optional supporting guides.

best_practices: Provide the learner profile, course goal, duration, and delivery format at the start.
Confirm the outline before asking for detailed lesson materials.
Share existing notes or constraints so the generated course matches real needs.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for full courseware before the lesson structure is agreed.
Do not use it as the only review for technical or regulated subject accuracy.
Do not omit audience level, because the pacing and examples depend on it.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates course outlines and lesson materials through a guided planning discussion.

faq.question: Can it make slide decks?
faq.answer: It can draft slide-ready content, but it does not create presentation files directly.

faq.question: Does it support different planning speeds?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports fast co-creation and stricter confirmation at each step.

faq.question: What information should I provide first?
faq.answer: Provide the topic, audience, course duration, desired output, and any existing notes.

faq.question: Can it revise an existing course?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review an existing outline and suggest a clearer flow, timing, and materials.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill contains documentation instructions only and no executable code.
